Chronology of every recorded fact about [[Dragon's Fang]], in reading order. Generated from the same verified fact store as the main article.

== House of Blades ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 9''' — A Dragon's Fang is a sword forged by the Wanderer and given to each of the twelve original soldiers of the Dragon Army.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 9''' — Only four of the thirteen swords (Dragon's Fangs) remain in use.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 10''' — Only four of the thirteen Dragon's Fangs are currently active.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 10''' — A Dragon's Fang is a weapon that can be summoned if its respect has been earned.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 11''' — Simon carries an undersized replica of a Dragon's Fang from the Valinhall armory.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 13''' — The trial is intended to ensure physical strength for one of the Dragon’s Fangs.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-01-house-of-blades|'''Ch 15''' — Simon sliced Erastes' bonds as easily as he could have with a Dragon’s Fang.}}

== The Crimson Vault ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 2''' — The battle-axe Kai used is not a Dragon's Fang.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 2''' — Dragon’s Fangs are gracefully curving swords forged by the Wanderer.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 4''' — The Dragon’s Fangs are made of Tartarus steel.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 4''' — The Dragon's Fang is used to assist in opening a Gate.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 4''' — Kai had recently had the Dragon’s Fang back.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 4''' — The Dragon's Fang is a sword used by Kai.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 9''' — Denner holds a Dragon's Fang.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 9''' — Kai's Dragon's Fang is in the Wanderer's hands.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 9''' — Dragon's Fang is a sword used by Indirial.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 10''' — The clash of Dragon's Fangs creates an ear-shattering noise.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 12''' — Simon and the Wanderer both possess Dragon's Fangs.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 15''' — A Dragon's Fang is something a chosen daughter can bear.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 18''' — A Dragon's Fang is required for easier traveling to the House.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 18''' — The thirteenth Dragon's Fang has an heir.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 18''' — The Dragon’s Fang is a weapon wielded by Denner.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 20''' — Dragon’s Fangs are lost artifacts.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 20''' — Andra wears a Dragon’s Fang buckled onto a belt.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-02-crimson-vault|'''Ch 20''' — A Dragon’s Fang is a weapon.}}

== City of Light ==
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 5''' — Dragon's Fang is a type of weapon or classification for Mithra.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 10''' — Ilana avoided the point of the Dragon’s Fang.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 13''' — Valinhall Travelers use Dragon's Fang swords.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 13''' — Dragon's Fangs are Valinhall-forged swords capable of cutting through trees, chains, and stone walls.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 13''' — A Dragon's Fang can split Alin's armor and penetrate an Incarnation's rib cage.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 14''' — Erastes' sword shines mirror-bright like one of the Dragon's Fangs.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 14''' — Andra's Dragon's Fang has a blade spattered with black spots like ink.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 14''' — A Dragon's Fang is a sword with a smooth vein of gold running down the flat of the blade.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 14''' — The Dragon's Fang is a blade that can push through polished armor.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 16''' — There are thirteen Dragon's Fangs.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 16''' — Six Dragon's Fangs are currently known to Simon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 16''' — Simon possesses one Dragon's Fang.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 16''' — Indirial possesses one Dragon's Fang.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 16''' — Andra possesses one Dragon's Fang.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 16''' — Kai possesses one Dragon's Fang.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 17''' — A Dragon's Fang is an object Simon was too weak to lift.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 19''' — Simon uses the Dragon’s Fang to strike Asphodel's neck.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 21''' — Indirial raises his Dragon’s Fang.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 21''' — Indirial intends to return the Dragon’s Fangs.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 21''' — The two Incarnations tossed Indirial three Dragon’s Fangs.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 21''' — Denner's Dragon's Fang has been removed.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 22''' — The edge of the Dragon's Fang scrapes at walls and doorframes.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 22''' — Ornheim gathered three Dragon’s Fangs.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 22''' — The Dragon’s Fang of Valin, the Wanderer, is a gold-and-silver weapon.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 22''' — Simon's grip closes around his Dragon's Fang.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 23''' — Indirial uses the Dragon’s Fang in his left hand.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 24''' — The Dragon's Fangs are capable of thinking for themselves.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 26''' — The Dragon's Fangs were taken hostage.}}
* {{spoiler|book=tg-03-city-of-light|'''Ch 27''' — The Dragon's Fang is an artifact held by Kathrin.}}

[[Category:Timelines]]
